characters.name: Aaron Lyle(Awakening)
characters.description: Aaron LyleInformationStatusDeceasedSpeciesHumanAppearancesSeasonsWestworld AwakeningFirst Seen"Rabbit's Snare"Last seen"Critical Incident"Death episode"Critical Incident"(off-screen)Appeared in2 episodesHistoryOccupationNarrative writerCause of deathChest opened with an axe byWyatt's gangPhysical AttributesAgeMid 20sGenderMaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairBlackEyeBrown Aaron Lyleis a minor character in VR gameWestworld Awakening. He isChelsea Brannon's co-worker. Lyle is a very stubborn person. He doesn't listen to Chelsea's points about what changes should be implemented and what should not.
characters.personality_section: Lyle is a very stubborn person. He doesn't listen to Chelsea's points about what changes should be implemented and what should not.

characters.name: Dolores Abernathy
characters.actor_name: Evan Rachel Wood(Prime, Christina and Connells copy)Tessa Thompson(Prime and Hale copy)Hiroyuki Sanada(Sato copy)Tommy Flanagan(Connells copy)Clifton Collins Jr.(Lawrence copy)
characters.description: Dolores AbernathyS1S2S2 (Hale)S3InformationAlso known asWyattWičhúŋt'e Aú(byGhost Nation)Deathbringer(by Ghost Nation)Lara Espin(pseudonym)Charlotte HaleChristinaID numberCH465517080StatusDolores Prime /Christina- Active[1]"Charlotte Hale" - Destroyed"Sato" - Inactive"Martin" - Inactive"Lawrence" - UnknownSpeciesHostActorEvan Rachel Wood(Prime, Christina and Connells copy)Tessa Thompson(Prime and Hale copy)Hiroyuki Sanada(Sato copy)Tommy Flanagan(Connells copy)Clifton Collins Jr.(Lawrence copy)AppearancesSeasons1,2,34(Hale copy and Christina)First Seen"The Original"Last seen"Que Sera, Sera"Death episode"Crisis Theory"Appeared in28 episodes(see below)HistoryOccupationLeader ofWyatt's gang(formerly)Executive director of theDelos Board(as Hale, formerly)RevolutionaryDate of creation2015Date of death2053Cause of deathErased after her memories are purged on the orders ofEngerraund SeracNarrative & BackstoryParkPark Five(formerly)Westworld(formerly)Narrative RoleCivilian(formerly)Rancher's daughterFamilyPeter Abernathy- Father(decommissioned)(Unnamed) - Mother(decommissioned)William- Former LoverTeddy Flood- Former LoverLiam Dempsey Jr.- Former Lover(deceased)Physical AttributesAgeEarly 30sMid 30s(as Hale)Late 50s(Sato copy)Mid 50s(Martin copy)Late 40s(Lawrence copy)GenderFemaleMale(Sato, Martin and Lawrence copies)EthnicityCaucasian-AmericanAfrican-American(as Hale)Asian-Japanese(Sato copy)Hispanic-American(Lawrence copy)HairBlondeBlack(as Hale; Sato and Lawrence copies)White & Gray(Martin copies)EyeBlueBrown(as Hale; Martin and Lawrence copies)Black(Sato copy) "Charlotte Hale" - Destroyed"Sato" - Inactive"Martin" - Inactive"Lawrence" - Unknown Tessa Thompson(Prime and Hale copy)Hiroyuki Sanada(Sato copy)Tommy Flanagan(Connells copy)Clifton Collins Jr.(Lawrence copy) Dolores's personality undergoes many changes and ultimately loops throughout the unfolding of her story. Dolores' scripted personality as she first emerges is defined by optimism, friendliness and empathy.  Caring, warm and kind, every morning in herloop, she awakes with a cheery disposition, takes her paints and easel downstairs and goes out on the porch to talk to her father, before heading into Sweetwater on an errand run for her (never seen) mother. During the first season, after her 'father' Peter Abernathy suffers a mental fragmentation on seeing a photograph from the outside world, he whispers the words 'These violent delights have violent ends' to her. Both the event and the words gradually triggering something in her.  Progressively she comes to question the world around her, and her outlook on life and sense of purpose evolve. After her father is murdered, she runs away instead of continuing along her usual story line. After she stumbles into Logan and William's camp, she follows them on theirbounty huntadventure. In the episode "Contrapasso" , Dolores is able to quickly kill several men (hosts) with a revolver. When asked by William how she did it, Dolores explains she no longe
characters.personality_section: Dolores's personality undergoes many changes and ultimately loops throughout the unfolding of her story. Dolores' scripted personality as she first emerges is defined by optimism, friendliness and empathy.  Caring, warm and kind, every morning in herloop, she awakes with a cheery disposition, takes her paints and easel downstairs and goes out on the porch to talk to her father, before heading into Sweetwater on an errand run for her (never seen) mother. During the first season, after her 'father' Peter Abernathy suffers a mental fragmentation on seeing a photograph from the outside world, he whispers the words 'These violent delights have violent ends' to her. Both the event and the words gradually triggering something in her.  Progressively she comes to question the world around her, and her outlook on life and sense of purpose evolve. After her father is murdered, she runs away instead of continuing along her usual story line. After she stumbles into Logan and William's camp, she follows them on theirbounty huntadventure. In the episode "Contrapasso" , Dolores is able to quickly kill several men (hosts) with a revolver. When asked by William how she did it, Dolores explains she no longer wants to be the 'damsel in distress', and that she is re-writing her own story.  However, it is revealed inThe Bicameral Mindthat the events around her journey with William and Logan happened 30 years prior to the prime events in Season 1, and that Dolores has previously broken her loop, qu

characters.name: Peter Abernathy
characters.description: Peter AbernathyOld PeterNew PeterInformationID numberSH0821201106PA553N63R1JK0124198334PR1012200710StatusDecommissioned - Old PeterDecommissioned - New PeterSpeciesHostActorLouis Herthum(Old Peter)Bradford Tatum(New Peter)AppearancesSeasons1,2First Seen"The Original"Last seen"Les Écorchés"Narrative & BackstoryParkWestworldNarrative RoleCannibal Cult Leader(formerly, Old Peter)Sheriff(formerly, Old Peter)RancherFamily(Unnamed) - WifeDolores Abernathy- Daughter(destroyed)Physical AttributesAgeLate 50sGenderMaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairBrownEyeBlue PA553N63R1JK0124198334PR1012200710 Decommissioned - New Peter Peter is a rancher, husband and father. He is kind man who loves his family. In his backstory he was a lawman before Dolores was born, and this appears to be consistent across all the hosts who inhabited the role for the 34 years she has been active. Nothing is known about the original hosts who inhabited the part of Dolores's father for the first 23 years of Escalante and then the Park's existence. Both of the hosts who we 'see' playing Peter have inhabited other parts prior to taking on the part of her father:

characters.name: Akane
characters.description: AkaneInformationAlso known asあかねアカネStatusUnknownSpeciesHostActorRinko KikuchiAppearancesSeasons2First Seen"Akane No Mai"Last seen"Phase Space"Appeared in2 episodesHistoryOccupationGeishaTeahouse owner(formerly)Narrative & BackstoryFamilySakura- Adopted Daughter(decommissioned)Physical AttributesAgeLate 30sGenderFemaleEthnicityAsian-JapaneseHairBlackEyeBrown アカネ Teahouse owner(formerly) As a copy of Maeve, Akane shares not only a protectiveness towards her companions and a strong personality, but also some level of self-awareness: she is able to disobey her narrative and kill the Shogun's emissary and later the Shogun himself, and is able to recognize Maeve as her counterpart when they first meet. Despite the fact that Sizemore more or less copied Maeve's code into Akane, there are some differences, as Akane shows no desire to leave the park. It is unclear if this is because Akane did not receive the same code that Ford gave Maeve or because Akane's own sentience, like Maeve, allowed her to make that choice.

characters.name: Akecheta
characters.description:
AkechetaSalesmanGhost NationInformationAlso known asGhost(by theHomestead Girl)Ake(byKohana)InjinID numberAK0000361457StatusDecommissioned(consciousness active in theValley Beyond)SpeciesHostActorZahn McClarnonAppearancesSeasons2,4First Seen"Reunion"Last seen"Metanoia"HistoryOccupationArgos Initiativesalesman(formerly)Tribesman(formerly)Leader of theGhost NationNarrative & BackstoryFamilyKohana- LoverPhysical AttributesAgeEarly 40sGenderMaleEthnicityNative-AmericanHairBlackEyeBrown Injin Tribesman(formerly)Leader of theGhost Nation The personality Akecheta had while pitching his idea toLogan Deloswas relaxed and confident, resembling a business man.
When his narrative got changed, Akecheta became cruel and fearless. However, after regaining some memories of his past life, he becomes softer and finally reaches consciousness.

characters.personality_section:
The personality Akecheta had while pitching his idea toLogan Deloswas relaxed and confident, resembling a business man.
When his narrative got changed, Akecheta became cruel and fearless. However, after regaining some memories of his past life, he becomes softer and finally reaches consciousness.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: AngelaSeason 1Season 2InformationStatusDestroyedSpeciesHostActorTalulah RileyAppearancesSeasons1,2First Seen"Chestnut"Last seen"Les Écorchés"Death episode"Les Écorchés"HistoryOccupationMember ofWyatt's gangCause of deathGrenade explosion caused by herself(suicide)Narrative & BackstoryParkWestworld(formerly)Narrative RoleArgos Initiativerepresentative(formerly)Townswoman(formerly)Guest Greeter(formerly)Cult MemberPhysical AttributesAge30sGenderFemaleEthnicityCaucasian-BritishHairBlondEyeHazel Townswoman(formerly)Guest Greeter(formerly)Cult Member –Angela , in "Chestnut", "Reunion" and "Les Écorchés" Angela herself describes herCornerstone(that part of her around her personality is built)  as being 'To always leave them wanting more'.  As such her personality is always somewhat aloof and mercurial, hard to grab hold of.   She also details her own programmed personality "Sexy, but not threatening.  Accommodating, but not unchallenging.  Sweet, but not boring.  Smart, but not intimidating." Angela is, it is revealed, the first host effectively 'pimped' out by Ford and Weber to get the money they want to develop the Park.  And is chosen to do so instead of Dolores, who is Arnold's favourite.  In the wake of this, on Dolores seeing Angela dressing, an interesting moment occurs between the two fellow female hosts.  Almost like a mutual recognition of the future that awaits them. It is not known what relationship, if any, the two had in Escalante, but Dolores recalls her, smiling at her, both of them walking freely around the town while other hosts are being trained. During the massacre that followed, as instigated by Arnold, Angela is seen grieving hugely over a young dapper male host who has been killed, before she herself is killed by Teddy. Despite this, on achieving her self-awareness, it is Dolores as Wyatt and Teddy that she is foursquare behind.  As a stalwart Lieutenant of Dolores/Wyatt, she leads the followers out in the Park that Robert has purposed, and makes every effort to coax Teddy back to Wyatt's side, foretelling that he will be at Wyatt's side again as he once was. In many ways, perhaps not surprisingly given her forced prostitution role for decades, Angela is the most zealous in search of revenge, and in service of the cause of Host freedom.  Unquestioning of Dolores's path, she is relentlessly vicious and single minded, frequently having to be reigned in by Dolores.  And, after Dolores reprograms Teddy to be a cold blooded killer, openly smirking when he shoots a QA man point blank in an act that even stuns Dolores herself. In the ultimate indicator of her zealotry, Angela sacrifices herself to take out theCradle, the very act of which ensures that she can't be brought back.

As such her personality is always somewhat aloof and mercurial, hard to grab hold of.   She also details her own programmed personality "Sexy, but not threatening. characters.name: Armistice
characters.description: ArmisticeArmisticeSnake TattooInformationStatusDecommissionedSpeciesHostActorIngrid Bolsø BerdalAppearancesSeasons1,2First SeenThe OriginalLast seenThe PassengerAppeared in11 episodesNarrative & BackstoryParkWestworld(formerly)Narrative RoleBanditFamily(Unnamed) - Mother(narrative, deceased)Physical AttributesAge30sGenderFemaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairWhiteEyeBlue –Armistice, "The Bicameral Mind" Armisticewas aHostin theroleof a bandit who is member ofHector Escaton's gangto raidSweetwater. She was played by Norwegian actressIngrid Bolsø Berdal. Armistice is a cold-blooded killer, her life shaped by her family's brutal murder and her hunt for the men who did it. As a character, she has shown no problems with shooting random people in the street to make a point, and may subscribe to Hector's existential theory that everything in life will end badly. However, Armistice shows strength and control. When Hector is not around, she controls the other men of his gang with little more than a look or hand motion. Her only moment of hesitation is when a gun is pointed at her in the hands of aGuest.

characters.name: Arroyo
characters.description: ArroyoInformationStatusDeceasedSpeciesHumanActorAustin PriesterAppearancesSeasons2First Seen"Virtù e Fortuna"Last seen"Virtù e Fortuna"Death episode"Virtù e Fortuna"Appeared in1 episodeHistoryOccupationMember of theWestworld QA Security ForceCause of deathShot dead byDoloresPhysical AttributesAge40sGenderMaleEthnicityAfrican-AmericanHairBlackEyeBrownArroyois a minor character in HBO'sWestworldand a member of theWestworld QA Security Force. He is played byAustin Priester. Arroyo most likely had a background in either military, law enforcement or some other security based profession before joininf theWestworld QA Security Force. Arroyo assistedCharlotte Halein the retrival ofPeter Abernathyduring the Attack at Forlorn Hope. He and another member of QA,Benson, entered the Fort, stunned Abernathy and managed escort him out of the fort. They didn't seem to noticeBernard Lowe. While leaving, they were spotted byDolores AbernathyandTeddy Flood, who chased them. Arroyo and two other QA members were shot down by Dolores, Hale and Benson managed to flee the scene with Abernathy.

characters.name: Banker(1973)
characters.description: BankerInformationStatusAliveSpeciesHumanActorDick Van PattenAppearancesFirst SeenWestworld (film)Last seenWestworld (film)HistoryOccupationBankerPhysical AttributesAgeLate 30sGenderMaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairBrownEyeBrown –The Banker after taking over as the new sheriff, just before being ridiculed upon by a random villager TheBanker(name unknown)is one of the 4 guests introduced in the 1973 filmWestworld. He is portrayed by Dick Van Patten. The Banker is a timid, non-confident and clumsy person, like theMedievalworld guest, who develops his character traits in the park, becoming more assertive and bold. It is apparent that he's never been in difficult situations before and he can't stand up for himself. As he's a banker, it's likely that he never does something himself since he can get others to do that due to his wealth and position. Unlike the Medievalworld guest, Banker's actions aren't antagonistic since he's empathetic — he doesn't abuse therobots, doesn't hurt them fatally as, for example,Johndoes.

characters.name: Old Bill
characters.description: Old BillInformationStatusDecommissionedSpeciesHostActorMichael WincottAppearancesSeasons1First Seen"The Original"Last seen"Contrapasso"Appeared in2 episodesNarrative & BackstoryParkWestworldNarrative RoleBartenderPhysical AttributesAgeLate 60sGenderMaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairWhite & GrayEyeBrown – Old Bill Old Billis a minor in thefirst seasonofWestworld. One of the firsthostsbuilt[1], Bill is now decommissioned and stored onSub-Level B83; he is still visited from time to time by the Park Director, Dr.Robert Ford. Bill is played byMichael Wincott. As an outmoded first-generation Host, Bill's personality seems to be limited. His only appearances involve him pouring and conversing over drinks; presumably this was part of his narrative when he was still in the park. He appears to be amiable and friendly toFord, or perhaps, to whomever shares a drink with him. His tone is wistful and somewhat detached, and he'll listen intently to stories that another person tells him, but only remarks that the story is "a real humdinger" — indicating that his listening, comprehension, and improvisation abilities are too primitive for anything else. Old Bill also exhibits a number of physical glitches including eyelids that blink asynchronously from each other, and he can't seem to execute even simple movements without momentary hitches. Despite, or perhaps because of, Old Bill's shortcomings, Dr. Ford appears to have a nostalgic affection for the host bartender. Old Bill will often indicate the end of a conversation loop by proposing the same toast in his affable, whiskey-and-tobacco-infused growl: "Shall we drink to the lady with the white shoes?" (The lady with the white shoes is referring toKate, the protagonist ofWestworld Awakening)

characters.description: Branding SoldierBranding soldierInformationSpeciesHostActorKaiwi Lyman-MersereauAppearancesSeasonsSeason OneFirst Seen"The Adversary"Physical AttributesAge30s-40sGenderMale TheBranding soliderwas a union soldier who attempted to brandTeddy FloodwithThe Mazeemblem.[1]

characters.name: Brigham
characters.description: BrighamInformationStatusDestroyedSpeciesHostActorFredric LehneAppearancesSeasons2,4First Seen"Virtù e Fortuna"Last seen"The Auguries"Death episode"Virtù e Fortuna"(flashback)Appeared in2 episodesHistoryOccupationColonel of theConfederadosCause of deathExplosion of dynamitePhysical AttributesAge50sGenderMaleEthnicityCaucasian-AmericanHairBlackEyeGreenBrighamis a minor character in HBO'sWestworld.He is a host and high ranking officer of theConfederados. Nothing is known about Brigham's narrative and story except that he is the leader of Confederados. Dolores and her posse await outside of a fort's walls as the fort's commandant — a colonel — approaches through the gates, accompanied by a fanfare and 21-gun salute. Dolores introduces herself to the colonel by saying, "Call me Wyatt." The colonel is doubtful, replying, "You'reWyatt?" TheConfederados' majorthat Dolores brought with her explains to the colonel that "Wyatt" knows of an attack from the east that will come in the morning. Dolores attempts to convince the colonel to combine his men with hers, and they'll be assured of victory against the coming invaders. A hardened military officer, who in the beginning did not trust Dolores but later
